verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass TM p24-family cargo receptor that cycles between ER, Golgi, and the plasma membrane as part of COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization alongside Golgi residency, and surface biotinylation studies have detected a PM pool. The dominant compartment is the early secretory pathway, but a documented minority surface pool from constitutive cycling places this in dual_localization rather than endomembrane_resident-only. Not a canonical surface receptor, so not `yes`.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Size and accessibility of the PM pool is modest; uncertain whether the short extracellular/luminal GOLD domain at the surface is a tractable binder epitope.
